questions.q: Who founded the Sikh Empire in 1799 CE?
questions.explanation: The Sikh Empire was founded by Maharaja Ranjit Singh in 1799 CE.

questions.q: The Sikh Empire ruled over which region of India?
questions.explanation: It ruled the Punjab and surrounding regions of northwestern India.

questions.q: In what year was the Sikh Empire founded?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh founded the empire in 1799 CE when he took Lahore.

questions.q: The Sikh Empire unified what kind of smaller Sikh states or confederacies?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh unified the various Sikh misls into a single empire.

questions.q: The Sikh Empire was the last major Indian power to fall to whom?
questions.explanation: It was the last major Indian power to fall to the British.

questions.q: In what year did the Sikh Empire finally fall to the British?
questions.explanation: The empire was annexed by the British in 1849.

questions.q: The Sikh Empire was especially known for what military strength?
questions.explanation: It was known for a strong, modernised standing army.

questions.q: Maharaja Ranjit Singh is popularly remembered by which nickname?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh is famously called the Lion of Punjab (Sher-e-Punjab).

questions.q: The Sikh Empire's ruling religion and identity was that of which faith?
questions.explanation: The empire was a Sikh state, though it ruled people of many faiths.

questions.q: The Sikh Empire's heartland centred on which historic city?
questions.explanation: Lahore was the capital and heart of the Sikh Empire.

questions.q: Which city served as the capital of the Sikh Empire?
questions.explanation: Lahore was the political capital of the Sikh Empire.

questions.q: Which famous diamond was held in the Sikh court before passing to the British in 1849?
questions.explanation: The Koh-i-Noor was held by the Sikh court and surrendered after annexation in 1849.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh modernised the Sikh army with the help of officers from where?
questions.explanation: He hired European officers, including French and Italian veterans, to modernise his army.

questions.q: The modernised regular infantry of the Sikh Empire was known as the what?
questions.explanation: The regular force was the Sikh Khalsa Army, with an elite corps called the Fauj-i-Khas.

questions.q: Which renowned general of the Sikh Empire expanded its frontier toward Peshawar and the northwest?
questions.explanation: Hari Singh Nalwa was a celebrated Sikh general who extended the frontier to the northwest.

questions.q: The Anglo-Sikh Wars that ended the empire were fought in which broad period?
questions.explanation: The two Anglo-Sikh Wars were fought in the 1840s, ending in 1849.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh captured which key city from the Afghans in 1818, extending the empire south?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh took Multan from Afghan control in 1818.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh's empire captured which strategic city near the Khyber region in 1834?
questions.explanation: The Sikh Empire captured Peshawar in 1834, pushing its frontier toward the Khyber.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh contributed gold to which famous shrine, giving it its golden appearance?
options: The Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib) at Amritsar
questions.explanation: He funded the gilding of the Harmandir Sahib, giving rise to the name Golden Temple.

questions.q: Ranjit Singh kept the British east of which river during his lifetime?
questions.explanation: By treaty, Ranjit Singh kept the British east of the Sutlej during his reign.

questions.q: The Sikh Empire was noted for what attitude toward its non-Sikh subjects?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh's rule was known for religious tolerance toward Hindus and Muslims alike.

questions.q: In what year did Maharaja Ranjit Singh die, after which the empire weakened?
questions.explanation: Ranjit Singh died in 1839, and the empire declined into instability afterward.

questions.q: The 1846 Treaty of Lahore, ending the First Anglo-Sikh War, ceded which region to the British?
options: Jalandhar Doab (and led to the sale of Kashmir)
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Lahore ceded the Jalandhar Doab and arranged the transfer of Kashmir to Gulab Singh.

questions.q: Who was the last Maharaja of the Sikh Empire, deposed when Punjab was annexed in 1849?
questions.explanation: The boy-king Duleep Singh was the last Maharaja, deposed at the 1849 annexation.

questions.q: The decisive battles of Chillianwala and Gujrat in 1849 belonged to which conflict?
questions.explanation: Chillianwala and Gujrat were fought during the Second Anglo-Sikh War of 1848 to 1849.

questions.q: Gulab Singh, who gained Kashmir after the First Anglo-Sikh War, founded which princely dynasty?
options: The Dogra dynasty of Jammu and Kashmir
questions.explanation: Gulab Singh founded the Dogra dynasty that ruled Jammu and Kashmir.

questions.q: Which European general organised the elite Fauj-i-Khas corps of the Sikh army?
questions.explanation: Jean-Baptiste Ventura, a French veteran, helped organise the elite Fauj-i-Khas.

questions.q: The Sikh Empire's currency was struck not in the ruler's name but in the name of whom?
questions.explanation: Coins were issued as Nanakshahi currency in the name of the Gurus rather than Ranjit Singh.

questions.q: At which 1837 battle did Hari Singh Nalwa fall while defending the empire's northwest frontier?
questions.explanation: Hari Singh Nalwa was killed at the Battle of Jamrud near the Khyber in 1837.

questions.q: Before unifying into the empire, the Sikh misls loosely coordinated through which institution?
questions.explanation: The misls coordinated through the Sarbat Khalsa assembly and the combined Dal Khalsa.
